header.title: Profile
header.description: Your personal information. Editable any time.

locale.footnote: Your personal amounts follow your currency. The interface is translated into the language you pick; languages without a complete translation fall back to English. The LIBERIA subscription is billed in CHF.
locale.unsupported: Country / currency / language combination not supported yet.

header.title: Settings
header.description: Tune your preferences and manage your data.

memory.intro: LIBERIA learns your profile to support you better.
memory.introSub: These preferences stay private and never leave your account. You can edit or wipe them any time.
memory.toneHelp: The tone LIBERIA uses when speaking to you.
memory.challengesHelp: Pick what resonates. Helps LIBERIA guide you better.
memory.triggersHelp: The moments when you spend more than planned.
memory.notesHelp: What you want LIBERIA to keep in mind about your trajectory. Only visible to you.
memory.notesPlaceholder: e.g. "I'm coming out of a rough patch, I need to move slowly."
memory.resetConfirm: Reset your coaching memory? Your financial data stays intact — only personalisation preferences are cleared.
memory.premiumIntro: As you talk to your coach, it remembers the things that matter (goals, preferences, life events). You stay in full control.

memoryPage.title: Premium AI memory
memoryPage.description: Everything your coach has remembered from your conversations. You can delete a single memory, turn memory off, or clear everything at any time.
memoryPage.howItWorksBody: After each exchange, the coach extracts up to 3 long-lasting memories and groups them into 4 categories:
memoryPage.howItWorksGoal: Goals: what you're trying to achieve financially.
memoryPage.howItWorksPreference: Preferences: how you want to be coached.
memoryPage.howItWorksEvent: Events: life changes that affect your budget.
memoryPage.howItWorksBlocker: Blockers: recurring obstacles to your progress.
memoryPage.toggleDescription: When disabled, the coach stops learning and stops using your existing memories.
memoryPage.toggleOnHelp: The coach learns from conversations and personalises its replies.
memoryPage.toggleOffHelp: Your existing memories stay stored but are no longer used.
memoryPage.entriesTitle: {count, plural, =0 {No memories} one {# memory} other {# memories}}
memoryPage.entriesDescription: Click the cross to delete a single memory without touching the rest.
memoryPage.empty: No memories yet. Keep talking to the coach — it will learn naturally.
memoryPage.clearAllConfirm: Permanently delete all your memories? This cannot be undone.

weekly.title: Weekly summary email
weekly.description: Every Sunday, a short recap of your financial week.

encouragement.title: Progress encouragement
encouragement.description: A short email when the coach detects notable progress.

milestones.title: Goal milestones
milestones.description: When a goal hits a tier (50 / 80 / 100%).

inactivity.title: Coach follow-up
inactivity.description: A gentle email after several inactive days.

trial.title: Trial and payment reminders
trial.description: Before the trial ends and on payment issues. Recommended.

analytics.title: Anonymous product analytics
analytics.description: Aggregated counters (never personal) to improve LIBERIA. No resale, no ad tracking.

subscription.intro: Manage your plan, billing and Premium access.

data.intro: Per GDPR, you can export or permanently delete all your data.
data.exportInfo: The export contains profile, income, expenses, goals, AI plans and conversation history.
data.deleteConfirm: Permanently delete your LIBERIA account? This action is irreversible and wipes all your data (income, expenses, goals, AI plans, conversations).

security.intro: Your data is encrypted at rest and in transit. Each user only accesses their own data — strict isolation enforced at the database layer.

header.title: Subscription
header.description: Manage your trial, plan and payment method.

banners.trialTitleWithDays: Free trial in progress — {days, plural, one {# day left} other {# days left}}
banners.trialBillingSoon: Automatic billing will start when the trial ends.
banners.trialCancelled: You've cancelled: no invoice will be charged.
banners.trialCanCancel: You can cancel any time via the portal.
banners.activeKeptUntilFallback: Your access continues until the end of the period.
banners.activeManaged: You can manage your subscription any time.
banners.pastDueBody: The last payment didn't go through. Update your payment method via the portal to reactivate access — your data stays safe.
banners.lapsedPausedBody: Pick a plan to reactivate full LIBERIA access. Your data stays intact.
banners.lapsedNewBody: Activate your {days}-day free trial. Cancel any time.

status.label: Current plan
status.trialUsedNote: You've already used your free trial. Future plans start at the standard rate.
status.freshNote: Start with {days} free days. Card required to activate.
status.manageNote: You can change plan or cancel any time via the portal.

timeline.title: Account activity
timeline.cancelScheduledDetail: You can revoke it via the portal while the period is running.

subscription.footnote: Billing in CHF for launch, whatever your country. Payment handled by Stripe (encrypted, PCI-compliant). Accepted methods: Visa / Mastercard / American Express cards, Apple Pay, Google Pay, and TWINT (Switzerland, depending on Stripe Dashboard activation).

coach.welcomeBody: Talk about your finances. The LIBERIA coach uses your income, expenses and goals to suggest concrete actions — calmly, without jargon, without promise.

chat.placeholderDemo: Demo mode: create an account to chat with the coach.
chat.disclaimer: The LIBERIA coach is informational. None of this is regulated financial advice.
chat.emptyBody: Ask a concrete question about your situation, your expenses or your goals. It uses your real data and proposes actions step by step.
chat.demoBanner: Demo mode: the conversation activates after creating an account.

default: What should I do this week?
How do I lower my financial stress?
Help me stick to my plan.
cashflowNegative: How do I close my monthly gap?
Which expense is the easiest to cut?
Where do I start to ease the pressure?
How do I get back some margin this month?
noEmergencyFund: How do I build an emergency fund at my pace?
What amount should I aim for to start?
How do I automate my savings?
Where do I start to feel safer?
debtHeavy: Which debt should I pay off first?
How do I lighten my debt load?
How do I prioritise between paying off and saving?
How do I renegotiate a loan?
anxious: How do I lower my financial stress?
Just one action to take today?
How do I find peace of mind around money?
Help me move forward without pressure.
impulsive: How do I curb impulse purchases?
How do I cap my variable spending?
How do I spot my spending triggers?
How do I build a habit that sticks?
solidBase: How do I accelerate my savings?
What goals should I aim for over 12 months?
How do I put my savings to work?
How do I optimise my insurance and contracts?

sidebar.demoToast: Demo mode: create an account to open a conversation.

openLine.gentle: {ack} No rush, we move at your pace.

closeLine.structured: Want me to detail one step in particular?
closeLine.gentle: Just tell me how you feel about all this.
closeLine.calm: Want to dig into one point in particular?

greeting.statusNegative: you're currently **{gap} short** of your monthly expenses — it's measurable, so it's addressable.
greeting.statusEmpty: we can start as soon as you've entered your income and expenses.
greeting.promptStructured: We can review 1 priority, 1 risk or 1 action — your call.
greeting.promptDefault: How can I help you today?

thanks.direct: My pleasure. Keep going — consistency does it all.
thanks.structured: My pleasure. Remember to note the outcome in LIBERIA so the trace stays.
thanks.gentle: Thank YOU. Take your time, I'm here whenever you need.
thanks.calm: My pleasure. We pick up when you want.

nextAction.cashflowNegative: Your current priority is to **reduce your monthly gap**. Today, identify **one single recurring expense** you can lower by about **{cut}** — a subscription, a delivery, a shopping category. You don't need to change everything, just place **a first lock**.
nextAction.highDti: Your repayments are **{pct}%** of your income — that's what's weighing the most right now. This week, list your loans by interest rate and concentrate your next efforts on the most expensive one.
nextAction.noFund: You don't have an emergency fund yet. That's your first milestone. Schedule **an automatic transfer of {target}** at the start of the month to a separate account. Symbolic at first, structural later.
nextAction.lowSavings: Your savings rate is low. This week, **review your active subscriptions** and cut those you haven't used this month. That's the action with the best effort / impact ratio.
nextAction.habit: You have a healthy base. **Pick a habit to test for 30 days**: an automatic transfer, a weekly shopping cap, or a Sunday evening financial review.

stress.ackDirect: Financial stress is measurable and reducible.
stress.ackStructured: Got it. We'll break down what's weighing.
stress.ackCalm: I get it. Having money pressure is hard — and very common.
stress.bodyCashflow: The most likely trigger of your stress is the gap between income and expenses: currently **{gap} more** than what comes in. We can tackle this **one expense at a time**, not all at once.
stress.bodyNoFund: Without a reserve, every surprise becomes a crisis. The best way to lower your stress is to **start an emergency fund** — even symbolic. A cushion of a few hundred already changes the daily feel significantly.
stress.bodyMental: You're not in financial red zone, but the mental load can still weigh. A short weekly financial review (10 minutes) is often enough to mentally regain control.

subscription.ack: Good lead — that's often where the most small leaks hide.
subscription.body: **Quick method**: open your last 3 bank statements, highlight every recurring line, and ask yourself ONE question per line — "Did I use it this month?". Cut without hesitation those you answer no — you can always resubscribe. Aim for **1 subscription cut this week**, not 5 — consistency beats performance.

savings.bodyNegative: To be able to save, you first need to **free up take-home** — trying to put aside when you're negative creates guilt without results. Let's focus on cutting an expense first, then we talk about savings.
savings.bodyPositive: Your take-home is **{cashflow}/month**. If you start modestly with **{target}** automatically transferred at the start of the month to a separate account, you build the habit without feeling it. That automatic move is what makes the difference at 12 months.

debt.ack: Important to talk about it clearly.
debt.bodyHigh: Your repayments are **{pct}%** of your income — above 30%, it's heavy. Strategy: **list your loans by interest rate** (from most expensive to least) and concentrate extra repayments on the most expensive. The others stay at minimum until that one is paid off.
debt.bodyManageable: Your repayments are **{pct}%** of your income — manageable. As long as it's under 25%, you can keep balancing repayment and savings. Above that, repayment first.
debt.bodyNone: You don't have any repayment declared in LIBERIA right now. If you have an ongoing loan that isn't logged yet, add it on the Expenses side so we can track it together.

budget.bodyData:
You have **{cashflow}** of take-home per month ({rate}% of your income). {tail}

For a cleaner budget, I suggest **3 mental envelopes**:
- Fixed (rent, bills, insurance)
- Variable (groceries, transport, going out)
- Set aside (savings, accelerated debt)

This week, just look at how much each envelope actually weighs.
budget.tailPositive: That's your room to manoeuvre — that's what we build with.
budget.tailNegative: The gap is negative — we'll work on reducing it before anything else.
budget.bodyEmpty: I don't have your income and expenses yet. Enter them in LIBERIA — even an estimate is enough to start.

goal.ack: Having a heading is what makes the difference.
goal.emergencyWithAmount: Your first reasonable milestone: **1 month of expenses** set aside (which is around **{monthlyExpenses}** in your case). Once reached, we aim for 3 months, then 6. Step by step.
goal.emergencyNoAmount: Your first reasonable milestone: **1 month of expenses** set aside. Once reached, we aim for 3 months, then 6. Step by step.
goal.debtPayoff: Your goal is to reduce debt. Set yourself a measurable tier on 90 days (e.g. close a credit card) rather than a total goal — it's more motivating.
goal.generic: Define your goal with **3 criteria**: a target amount, a deadline, and a monthly amount to set aside. Without these 3, the goal stays fuzzy. Set them in LIBERIA and we tackle it.

unclear.ackDirect: So we cut to the essentials, two options.
unclear.ackDefault: No worries. We can start from a simple angle.
unclear.summaryEmpty: Your financial data isn't filled in yet — we can start there.
unclear.fundNoteMissing: and you don't have an emergency fund yet
unclear.options:
- Want to **cut an expense**? Say "which expense to watch this week".
- Want to **start saving**? Say "how to build an emergency fund".
- Want to **manage debt**? Say "what to pay off first".
- Want **a general check-in**? Say "give me a quick recap".

header.title: Your financial plan
header.description: A concrete action plan to move forward week after week.

plan.aiNotReady: The AI plan is coming shortly.
plan.demoReason: Demo mode: create an account to generate a plan.
plan.demoEmptyTitle: The AI plan is reserved for accounts
plan.demoEmptyBody: Create your account to generate a personalised 30, 60 or 90-day plan based on your real data.

generate.description: The LIBERIA coach uses your real numbers (income, expenses, goals) to build a concrete action plan.
generate.archiveNotice: The current plan will be archived.
generate.footnote: Generation usually takes 10 to 30 seconds. You can close this window — the plan will appear as soon as it's ready.

30.label: 30 days
30.description: Short sprint: one clear, measurable goal.

60.label: 60 days
60.description: Two months to install new habits for the long run.

90.label: 90 days
90.description: Full quarter — the most solid transformation.

timeline.empty: No steps in this plan yet.

starter.fineprint: This plan is a frame. You'll be able to replace it with a personalised AI version once the coach is ready.
starter.demoReason: Demo mode: create an account to generate a plan.

summaries.struggling: Three months to ease the pressure, free up some breathing room and rebuild a first financial cushion. One step at a time, no rush.
summaries.tight: Three months to put an end to tight month-ends, build a first emergency fund and ease the costliest debts.
summaries.stable: Three months to consolidate your foundations, secure your savings and prepare a portion that works for you.
summaries.comfortable: Three months to refine your allocation, put your savings to work and concretely prepare a significant project.

common_w1_s1.title: List your 5 largest fixed expenses
common_w1_s1.description: Rent, insurance, subscriptions, transport. Record the exact amounts in LIBERIA — no judgement, just clarity.

common_w1_s2.title: Spot 1 subscription to cancel or renegotiate
common_w1_s2.description: Just one — the most obvious. Unused streaming, paused gym, duplicate insurance. Start small.

common_w2_s1.title: Log 7 days of daily expenses
common_w2_s1.description: Coffee, transport, groceries, outings. Nothing to change this week — just observe your real pattern.

common_w3_s1.title: Schedule an automatic transfer to a savings account
common_w3_s1.description: Even symbolic (20–50 CHF). The point is to build the reflex — the amount can grow later.

common_w4_s1.title: Set 1 concrete 90-day goal
common_w4_s1.description: Concrete and realistic: "500 CHF emergency fund", "pay off X of credit card". Activate it in the Goals section.

struggling_w5_s1.title: Contact your landlord or bank for a payment schedule
struggling_w5_s1.description: If a charge is squeezing you, propose a phased plan. Most agree — the worst answer is no.

struggling_w6_s1.title: Ask a social service or association for help
struggling_w6_s1.description: Caritas, regional social centre, Pro Senectute — many forms of help exist and remain confidential. There's nothing to lose by asking.

struggling_w7_s1.title: Pick 1 non-essential category to halve this month
struggling_w7_s1.description: Delivery, outings, clothing. Not from guilt — just to gain breathing room.

struggling_w8_s1.title: Cancel 1 additional subscription
struggling_w8_s1.description: By now you have better visibility — target the ones you pay out of habit without real value.

struggling_w9_s1.title: List 3 realistic side-income ideas
struggling_w9_s1.description: Extra hours, one-off mission, selling unused items. Activating just one is enough.

struggling_w10_s1.title: Activate 1 lead this week
struggling_w10_s1.description: Sign-up, message, listing. The hardest part is the first step — after that it flows.

struggling_w11_s1.title: Aim for 100 CHF emergency fund this week
struggling_w11_s1.description: Small milestone but symbolic. Your first cushion already lifts mental load.

struggling_w12_s1.title: Review: where are you vs. 90 days ago?
struggling_w12_s1.description: Calm review. Note 3 things that changed for the better. Set the next 90-day milestone.

tight_w5_s1.title: Renegotiate 1 contract (mobile, insurance, internet)
tight_w5_s1.description: Call your provider, ask for the latest loyalty offer. 15 minutes can free 20–80 CHF/month.

tight_w6_s1.title: Cap 1 variable category (leisure, shopping)
tight_w6_s1.description: Set a weekly budget. If you go over, it resets the following week — no guilt.

tight_w7_s1.title: Increase your automatic transfer by 10–20%
tight_w7_s1.description: If you saved 50 CHF, move to 60 CHF. Barely noticeable adjustment, important compounding effect.

tight_w8_s1.title: Physically separate your savings
tight_w8_s1.description: Different account, even a different bank. The further the money is from your current account, the less you touch it.

tight_w9_s1.title: List your debts by interest rate (most expensive first)
tight_w9_s1.description: Credit card or consumer loan often sits at the top. Target that one first.

tight_w10_s1.title: Shift one minimum payment toward faster repayment
tight_w10_s1.description: Even 50 CHF more on the costliest debt saves hundreds long term.

tight_w11_s1.title: Build 1 weekly review routine (10 min)
tight_w11_s1.description: Same day, same time. Open LIBERIA, look at your cash flow, adjust if needed.

tight_w12_s1.title: Set the next 90-day goal
tight_w12_s1.description: Reach 1 month of emergency fund? Pay off a loan? Choose 1 clear, quantified target.

stable_w5_s1.title: Aim for 3 months of emergency fund
stable_w5_s1.description: You have a base — the next step is long-term safety. Compute the target amount and set a deadline.

stable_w6_s1.title: Insurance audit: remove duplicates
stable_w6_s1.description: Home, private liability, travel. Many coverages overlap. A free consultation with an independent broker often suffices.

stable_w7_s1.title: Compare your savings account to the best offers
stable_w7_s1.description: If your rate is below 1%, look at what online banks offer. The difference is hundreds of CHF / year.

stable_w8_s1.title: Increase your automatic transfer by 10%
stable_w8_s1.description: You won't feel the difference in your cash flow, but over 12 months it's already a sizable extra cushion.

stable_w9_s1.title: Look into the 3rd pillar (Switzerland) or a long-term investment
stable_w9_s1.description: Inform yourself before investing. No rush. This week = reading only.

stable_w10_s1.title: Define your target allocation (short / medium / long term)
stable_w10_s1.description: How much do you keep liquid? How much can you lock for 5+ years? Set the ratios before acting.

stable_w11_s1.title: Set 1 quantified 12-month goal
stable_w11_s1.description: "6-month emergency fund", "3rd pillar opened and funded", "X CHF invested". Set it in Goals.

stable_w12_s1.title: 90-day review + next quarter plan
stable_w12_s1.description: Calm review. What worked, what you keep, what you change. You have the landmarks now.

comfortable_w5_s1.title: Compute your real savings capacity (vs. stated)
comfortable_w5_s1.description: You may save more than you think. Compare what leaves your account each month vs. what you believe you set aside.

comfortable_w6_s1.title: Define your target allocation (cash / invested / long term)
comfortable_w6_s1.description: What % for emergencies? For retirement? For 3–5-year projects? State it clearly.

comfortable_w7_s1.title: Open or fund a 3rd pillar (Switzerland)
comfortable_w7_s1.description: Tax optimisation + retirement. Check the annual cap based on your status.

comfortable_w8_s1.title: Compare the fees on your current investments
comfortable_w8_s1.description: 1% in excess fees over 20 years = tens of thousands of CHF less. Audit first.

comfortable_w9_s1.title: Define 1 quantified 1–3-year project
comfortable_w9_s1.description: Real estate purchase, sabbatical, business launch. Set the target amount and the deadline.

comfortable_w10_s1.title: Separate this project from the rest of your savings
comfortable_w10_s1.description: Dedicated account or fund. You see real progress and don't dip into it by mistake.

comfortable_w11_s1.title: Income audit: how dependent is your flow on a single source?
comfortable_w11_s1.description: Diversifying income matters as much as diversifying savings. Identify 1 realistic lever.

comfortable_w12_s1.title: 90-day review + annual horizon
comfortable_w12_s1.description: You have the base and the margin — set measurable annual goals and pick 2 priorities.

transaction.description: Enter the amount and its frequency. You can edit any time.
transaction.variableHint: This one-off expense will be dated to today.
transaction.label: Label

goal.description: Choose a tangible, reachable goal. You can adjust it.

list.demoDeleteError: Demo mode: sign in to delete.
list.deleteConfirmGoal: Delete the goal « {title} »? This is permanent.

list.emptyExpenseDescription: List your essential expenses then the non-essential ones.
list.emptyGoalsDescription: Set your first step: emergency fund, debt to pay off, project.

header.title: Your monthly budget
header.description: The overview: what comes in, what goes out, what's left to move forward.

budget.analyticsHint: {transactions, plural, =0 {No transactions this month} one {# one-off transaction this month} other {# one-off transactions this month}} — see fixed vs variable, budgets and opportunities in /expenses/analytics.

analytics.title: Detailed expense analytics
analytics.description: Understand where your money goes and set per-category limits.

period.title: Period
period.description: Pick the analysis window. The "this month" numbers match the dashboard and coach exactly.

totals.totalHint: {transactions, plural, =0 {No transactions} one {# transaction} other {# transactions}}

breakdown.title: Breakdown by category
breakdown.description: Sorted by amount over the selected period.

budgets.title: Monthly budgets per category
budgets.description: Set a limit per category. The coach will surface overruns naturally.
budgets.empty: No budgets set. Add one below to track each category against a target.

performance.title: Budget performance
performance.description: For each budgeted category: target, actual, gap and status.

opportunities.title: Opportunities detected
opportunities.description: Computed from your actual data. High priority = look at this first.
opportunities.empty: Everything's under control this month. Keep going.

budget_over.title: {category} budget over
budget_over.body: You spent {amount} above your {limit} limit this month.

high_variable_share.title: High one-off spending
high_variable_share.body: Your variable spending is {share}% of your income this month ({variable}).

low_emergency_fund.title: Emergency fund too low
low_emergency_fund.body: You cover {months} months of expenses (target 3 months).

high_fixed_ratio.title: Very high fixed costs
high_fixed_ratio.body: Your fixed costs are {share}% of your income. Good time to audit insurance, subscriptions, telecom.

high_insurance_share.title: High insurance share
high_insurance_share.body: You pay {amount}/month in insurance ({share}% of income). Useful to compare premiums and check your deductible.

high_subscriptions_share.title: Subscriptions worth auditing
high_subscriptions_share.body: {amount}/month of subscriptions ({share}% of income). Good moment for a clean-up.

history.title: Category history
history.description: Month-by-month evolution over the chosen window.
history.empty: No expenses over the selected window.

header.description: Salary, freelance, allowances, side income — everything that comes in, sorted cleanly.

stats.sourcesHint: Add every source for a faithful calculation.

header.title: Your expenses

stats.linesHint: You can edit or delete them any time.
stats.openAnalytics: {transactions, plural, =0 {No transactions} one {# transaction} other {# transactions}} this month · open analytics

header.title: Your financial goals
header.description: One clear goal beats ten good intentions. Move forward step by step.

billing.fineprint: Card required to start. Automatic billing at the end of the {days} days. Cancel any time.

feedback.successBody: Your access is being activated, this can take a few seconds.
feedback.cancelled: Payment cancelled. You can retry any time.

monthly.name: Monthly
monthly.description: Full LIBERIA access. Cancel any time from your account.

yearly.name: Yearly
yearly.description: That's ~9.99 CHF/month — about 60 CHF saved per year.

unsubscribe.serviceUnavailableBody: Link-based unsubscribe isn't configured on this environment. Open your settings directly to manage your email preferences.
unsubscribe.invalidLinkBody: This unsubscribe link is incomplete. Open your settings in LIBERIA to manage your email preferences.
unsubscribe.doneBody: You'll no longer receive the LIBERIA weekly recap. You can reactivate it any time from your settings.
unsubscribe.errorBody: This unsubscribe link isn't recognised. Open your settings in LIBERIA to manage your email preferences.
unsubscribe.confirmBody: You'll no longer receive the LIBERIA weekly recap. You can reactivate it any time from your settings.

admin.intro: Internal counters — aggregates only, never individual data. No data is exposed to any third party.

readiness.title: Production readiness
readiness.missing: {count, plural, one {# required item missing} other {# required items missing}}
readiness.envNote: No env value is ever displayed — only the presence (yes/no) of each variable.

admin.note1: Counters reflect the current state (no time-series history). For series over time, plug in a dedicated analytics provider (see lib/analytics/).
admin.note2: No email, full name, amount or user content appears on this page. If you see any — it's a bug, please report it.

metadata.title: Demo mode
metadata.description: Explore LIBERIA with realistic data — no sign-up required.

banner.title: You're exploring LIBERIA in demo mode.
banner.body: The data shown is fictional. Create your account to pilot your real data (14-day free trial).

header.title: Demo dashboard
header.description: A representative view of someone rebuilding their finances.

shortTerm.label: Short term
shortTerm.body: Aim for 1 month of expenses in your emergency fund within 6 months.

automation.label: Automation
automation.body: Set up a recurring monthly transfer to savings, even a symbolic one.

calm.label: Calm and reassuring
calm.description: Soft, no pressure. Pace beats performance.
